title: Forty-Three in the Shade

mood.label: Uneasy calm 🌥️
mood.summary: A holding Gulf ceasefire and oil sliding to a three-month low gave the day real relief, but a record-breaking, deadly European heat dome, a worsening Ebola epidemic in DR Congo and a widening Sudan war pinned the mood just below neutral.

style.genre: industrial-tinged downtempo / dub — a sampled clarinet tracing a dark, circling topline over a dub-spaced, slightly metallic RhythmAce kit with deep tape-echo, a processed muted-guitar pulse, a sine sub and a bowed-string drone underneath; C harmonic minor's raised seventh dragging hard against the lowered sixth, an uneasy tension under a surface that refuses to break either way

sampleSources: Strudel default library (VCSL, RhythmAce, sampled piano)
pattern:
stack(
  s("bd ~ ~ rim ~ ~ sd ~, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ hh ~ hh").bank("RhythmAce").gain(0.62).hpf(180).room(0.28).delay(0.22).delaytime(0.375).swingBy(0.08, 8),
  note("<[c3,ef3,g3,b3] [af2,c3,ef3] [g2,b2,d3,f3] [f2,af2,c3]>").s("clarinet").attack(0.04).release(0.6).lpf(sine.range(700,1900).slow(12)).gain(0.4).room(0.4).rsize(4).pan(0.42),
  note("~ g4 ~ af4 b4 ~ c5 ~ ~ b4 ~ g4 af4 ~ ~ ~").s("clarinet").lpf(2600).gain(0.34).delay(0.34).delaytime(0.5).room(0.3).off(0.125, x => x.gain(0.18).pan(0.7)),
  note("c2 ~ c2 g2 ~ af1 ~ f2").s("sine").lpf(420).gain(0.5).attack(0.02).release(0.3),
  note("<c4 g3>").s("gm_acoustic_guitar_nylon").struct("x ~ x ~ ~ x ~ x").lpf(sine.range(900,2400).slow(16)).gain(0.22).distort(0.6).delay(0.28).delaytime(0.25).pan(0.6).hpf(300),
  note("<c2 af1 g1 f1>").s("sawtooth").attack(1.4).release(3).lpf(700).gain(0.16).room(0.5).jux(rev)
)

lines: Forty-three in the shade and the river's full of strangers
trying to stay alive in the water by the bridge,
the Louvre shut its doors, the red alerts kept ringing,
and the heat just leaned its weight on every roof and ridge.
Down in Darfur a span goes dark over the wadi,
in the Congo the count climbs past a thousand and beyond,
but the tankers crossed the strait like nothing ever happened,
and the price of a barrel slipped, and the world moved on.
We're holding on the line, neither sinking nor climbing,
one hand on the relief, one hand on the dread,
the ceasefire's still standing while the summer's still burning —
an uneasy calm, just below the neutral thread.
A senate found its nerve, a courtroom found its verdict,
Pakistan keeps wiring sunlight to the grid,
and somewhere in the dark of the seven-hundred-million-year sky
a quasar flickers on for whoever's still a kid.
